faqs.a: Histrionic personality disorder is a long-standing pattern of excessive emotionality and attention-seeking that begins by early adulthood and shows up across many situations. People with it often feel uncomfortable when they aren't the center of attention. It's one of the personality disorders described in the DSM-5-TR.

faqs.q: How is it different from narcissistic personality disorder?
faqs.a: Both involve a strong pull toward attention, but they differ. Histrionic personality disorder centers on emotional expressiveness and a need to be noticed and approved of, while narcissistic personality disorder centers more on grandiosity and a sense of superiority. They're separate diagnoses, though they can overlap.

faqs.q: Can histrionic personality disorder be treated?
faqs.a: Yes. Psychotherapy is the main treatment, and many people make meaningful progress over time. A personality disorder is diagnosed by a qualified clinician, never from a checklist alone, so an evaluation is the right first step if the pattern is causing distress.
